questions.prompt: Which among the following novels combined African American folklore, history, and literary tradition to celebrate the moral and spiritual revival of Macon Dead, the first male protagonist in a Morrison novel?
Who among the following was the first black American woman to win the 1993 Nobel Prize for Literature?
Who among the following critics coined the term ‘queer theory’?
Which among the following fictional characters of Toni Morrison wished for blue eyes for the sake of 'beautifying herself'?
Which among the following novels by Toni Morrison won the Pulitzer Prize for fiction?
Which among the following critical works is authored by Nancy Chodorow?
In Toni Morison’s novel Beloved (1987), which character kills her daughter in order to save her from a life of slavery and misery?
Which of the following propositions were made by Teresa De Lauretis in her work Technologies of Gender (1987) while considering her theories on gender?

options.B: The construction of gender goes on today through various technologies of gender and institutional discourses.
options.C: The representation of gender is its construction. Its construction goes on in public and private spaces, in media, also in ideology.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not one of Teresa de Lauretis’s four propositions?

options.D: Gender construction is affected by its deconstruction. It goes on today through various technologies of gender and institutional discourses.

questions.prompt: Who famously said “anatomy is destiny,” linking gender to biological sex?
Who among the following wrote The Wilde Century (1994), which explored how notions of “queerness” were closely linked to upper-class effeminacy through the 20th century?
In which year did Gayle Rubin publish her critical essay “The Traffic in Women: Notes on the 'Political Economy”?
Which of the following is a stage of Coming Out according to Cass Theory developed by Vivian Cass?

options.A: Identity Tolerance: The acceptance of one's homosexuality increases, and people begin to tolerate their identity.
options.B: Identity Acceptance: A sign of identity acceptance is when the person feels more connected around people from the LGBTQIA+ community and prefers being with them more than cisgenders.

questions.prompt: The antonym of the word transgender is ________
Who wrote the dissertation entitled The Valley of Kings: Leathermen in San Francisco (1960–1990) that was hailed as the most authoritative history of gay men's leather in San Francisco?
Which of the following are features of non-Binary gender/Genderqueer?

options.A: It is an umbrella term used to describe people who feel their gender cannot be defined within the margins of the gender binary.
options.B: They understand their gender in a way that goes beyond simply identifying as either a man or woman.

questions.prompt: Who among the following wrote the book Between Men: English Literature and Male Homosocial Desire (1985) that examines the power dynamics of the erotic triangle of two men and one woman found throughout British literature?
Which among the following essays inspired Eve Sedgwick to write her seminal work Epistemology of the Closet (1990)?

options.A: Gretel Ehrlich's essay, "The Solace of Open Spaces" (1985)
options.B: D. A. Miller's essay, "Secret Subjects, Open Subjects" (1988)

questions.prompt: Which famous essay by Gayle Rubin begins with the argument that sexuality is not a frivolous diversion from the more critical problems of poverty, war, disease, racism, or nuclear annihilation?

options.A: The Traffic in Women: Notes on the 'Political Economy' of Sex
options.B: Thinking Sex: Notes for a Radical Theory of the Politics of Sexuality

questions.prompt: In Proust’s In Search of Lost Time, Eve Sedgwick identifies Baron de Charlus as:
Who coined the term "Womanism" to refer to "African- American feminism or the feminism of women of color"?
Maria Stewart, in her speeches, compared African women to:
Which of the following is not a Slave Narrative?

options.A: Interesting Narrative of the Life of Olaudah Equiano (1789)
options.B: The Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass (1845)
options.C: Incidents in the Life of a Slave Girl (1861)

questions.prompt: Who among the following defined Feminism as a "movement to end sexism, sexist exploitation, and oppression"?
Which of the following stereotypical figures presents Black women as obedient and faithful domestic servants who care for white children and family better than her own?
Which of the following books is not written by bell hooks?

options.B: Talking Back: Thinking Feminist, Thinking Black (1989)
options.C: Sisters Of The Yam: Black Women and Self-recovery (1993)

questions.prompt: Which among the following is the stereotypical image linked to the working-class Black women’s increasing access to the US welfare state entitlements? The image presents a mother who is lazy, who is content to collect welfare, shunning work and also passing on her corrupt values to her own children.
Which among the following are the characteristics of Intersectionality?

options.A: Intersectionality is a framework for conceptualizing a person, group of people, or social problem as affected by several discriminations and disadvantages.
options.B: It asserts that people are often disadvantaged by multiple sources of oppression - their race, class, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, and other identity markers, which result in interdependent systems of impairment.

questions.prompt: The film Madea’s Big Happy Family (2011) perpetuates which controlling stereotype?
Who said, “Black feminism is not a white feminism in blackface”?
Which theorist is associated with the concept of the cyborg in feminist posthumanism?
Ihab Hassan's 1977 work is significant because it:
Haraway’s cyborg challenges which traditional binary?
The cyborg represents:

options.A: A world free of technological influence
